reviews.text: DON'T GIVE ELKE ANY MONEY UP FRONT! I retained Elke on Oct 3, 2019 to file a divorce petition for me and I paid her a retainer for the whole expected case. One year later, my wife and I reconciled. That was four years ago! I have been asking Elke ever since then to file for a dismissal of my petition. I have called Elke multiple times each year and she has said that she will do it, but she still has not. Sometimes Elke says that she will send me something to sign, and she doesn't. Other times Elke has said that she will get back to me in a day or two and never has. Sometimes Elke says that my wife and I will need to sign something together, which we don't. I paid a paralegal who told me that he can't help me with a dismissal because Elke is the one with my power of attorney and that Elke has the power to file for the dismissal herself. I am retaining a paralegal to assist me in writing and submitting a complaint to The State Bar of California. My wife and I love each other, but four years later we still don't have an end to the pending divorce case, nor a refund of our unused retainer. LEARN FROM MY EXPERIENCE!
reviews.ownerResponse: A dismissal was filed years ago as requested.

She approached every aspect of the case with thoroughness and precision, employing effective techniques that ultimately led to a successful resolution. Her attention to detail and strategic thinking made a significant difference in the outcome, and I felt supported throughout the entire process.

Additionally, Attorney Felicia Williams assisted me with my estate planning needs, demonstrating her versatility and deep understanding of the law. She guided me through the complexities of establishing a trust, ensuring that my wishes were clearly articulated and legally sound. Her proactive approach and clear communication made what could have been a daunting task feel manageable and straightforward.

I wholeheartedly recommend Attorney Felicia Williams at Williams Law Firm to anyone seeking legal assistance, whether for civil matters or estate planning. Her professionalism, expertise, and dedication to her clients set her apart in the legal field. If you are looking for an attorney who will advocate for you with integrity and skill, Attorney Felicia Williams is the one to choose.

What sets Felicia apart is her genuine care for her clients. She takes the time to understand the unique needs of each situation, offering personalized solutions that ensure success. Beyond her legal acumen, her kindness, approachability, and willingness to go above and beyond make her an absolute pleasure to work with. She's not just a fantastic attorney—she’s someone who truly invests in the people she works with, and that makes all the difference.

Eric Symonds helped me through one of the most difficult periods of my life during a child custody and divorce case. From our very first meeting, he took the time to clearly explain legal realities and details that no other attorney had, immediately giving me reassurance and confidence in the decisions I was making for myself.

What truly sets Eric apart is his empathy and genuine understanding of what individuals and families experience during custody disputes and divorce. Having worked with multiple attorneys before, I can confidently say he truly cares about his clients and understands both sides of the family court system, whether you are a mother or a father.

During an incredibly uncertain time, Eric gave my entire family a renewed sense of peace and stability. When I was close to losing hope, he stepped up in a big way, clearly outlined how he would advocate for me in court, and followed through every step of the way. He represents his clients as if they were family, delivers in the courtroom, and consistently advocates for outcomes that are truly in his clients’ best interests.

Having been a resident of Southern California, now 30 years. I strongly encourage anyone seeking a family law attorney not to waste time looking elsewhere. Take my word, along with the many five-star reviews—you will find no better family law attorney than Eric.

Highly recommend Eric Symonds — especially in family law. Been with him for 2 years for my Child Custody case that evolved into a DVRO case against me to keep alienated from daughter. Debra, his paralegal, is also extremely prompt on whatever is needed to be booked or filed. Eric has always been extremely up front with me on what I needed hear, and do. I now walk out with more court ordered weekly visits, and overnights with my daughter after every hearing. I owe the world to Eric for his efforts keeping my daughter in my life.

Eric is absolutely outstanding and will fight for you like you're family! I can't say enough nice things! He has helped me twice now and I am beyond grateful for all his and his staff's hard work! A big thank you to Debra and Barbara as well!

Mr Symonds office staff has stellar costumer service, respectful , friendly and helpful and his service affordable . You will not be disappointed! They’re like family now!

When bringing up concerns about not being represented as previously stated; snarky comments and insulting comments are made to the point where I got up from his office and demanded him to be removed as my counsel immediately. A perfect example of this are his responses to bad reviews. Not apologizing for mistakes but instead “I’m sorry you feel this way”, “things are being twisted and are not true.” I was represented by Eric for approximately a year and a half. Initially he stated that it wouldn’t be more than 3 months and a couple court dates till I would be able to get visitation with my daughter, which at that point I would no longer need representation and would be given the remaining amount of the retainer. Well 5k later, 1.5 years and 7 court dates (maybe more) we had gotten no where with only having 45m conjoined therapy and monitored visits 2 times a month (which during the consultation he claimed would never happen and wouldn’t be necessary). Mind you I work for a school district and have two step children I care for frequently. The supervisor for monitored visits stated that she has never seen this happen in 20 years and was dumbfounded if the situation and didn’t understand how it went this way. When I told Eric this he got super angry and defensive and started bashing her for giving her opinion, stating she is not a lawyer and can’t give legal advise (not legal advice lol!). I should have hired her as a lawyer as she has a better understanding of family law than Eric claims. Finally, I would stray away from this man, his temper, lack of knowledge, forgetfulness, no communication, no transparency, will roll over and will not fight for you, drain your retainer, very unorganized and careless, will not follow through with what he says, the list can go on just read his reviews. Hopefully, with all the other clients he’s had that were treated poorly, others can save some money and time by seeking out other representation.
Simply and amazing lawyer, caring and thoughtful Eric and Barbara are wonderful

Even calling me from home on a Friday night to give me the good news so I didn't have to stew all weekend! THANK YOU!!!

My apologies folks. This is unpleasant for me. But it needs to be done..
I cannot fully describe just how terrible my experience here was. So much so. That it eventually drove me to depression and an attempt on my own life.
I turned to this man to assist my child with help escaping her abusive mother. My daughter told him what happened. I explained what I had been through. And my daughter's mother gave her version filled with lies. This man not only sided with the woman spreading lies. He began to accuse me of coaching my daughter. At one point yelling at both my daughter and myself. Accusing us of making the whole thing up. The court appointed him to protect my daughter. Instead he ushered her into her mother's custody. And caused me so much mental distress that it drove me to give up my custody and later was a large part behind my own suicide attempt.
You see. My daughter was telling the truth. And a recent dcfs case has exposed this man's inadequacies. Yes. At the end of last year my daughter's mother beat her AGAIN. Brutally.
And all of the abuse that I had reported was found to be true. Just like I had reported.
My life now ruined due to all of this. My daughter in a foster home. Why? Because when I went to her defense this man not only refused to defend her. But attacked me. And I was forced to give up my rights I had fought so hard to defend.
I'm sorry. To anyone using him currently. I advise you to seek other council. And to those thinking of using him. Please do not.
I beg of you.
Let my experiences be a warning of the loudest kind.

This speaks to his character, he can't even stick to his initial response without changing his tune. He appears to be unstable and very egotistical. I have witnessed the threatening body language that was mentioned in another review. In his prior responses (that were later deleted) he admitted to spending Sunday night responding to these reviews when he had a case to work on!! I am completely floored that he would even admit to something like that, which is why I'm sure he deleted it days later. Mr. Seymonds, rather than reflecting on his performance, resorts to "victim" mode and accuses reviewers of a smear campaign (another response that was later deleted). This is beyond baffling. I personally, would never use this attorney for any matter, whatsoever. I know the prior clients that he very poorly represented, and the attorney-client privacy privileges were continually violated by Mr. Seymonds. For example, he discussed the case via a public website. Do you want an attorney who would be willing to discuss your case on the web!?!?? I wouldn't. These are my personal opinions based on my observations of Mr. Seymonds. I hope that he is not allowed to hurt another family again.

reviews.text:
To future clients of Eric W...I would like to address some facts...
Eric W enough w/the derogatory statements i.e Chip on his shoulder the size of the N.D, whatever your struggling thru at this point in Life...let's get to the facts.....no one is gauging your abilities as a Lawyer, we are just stating the facts from previous reviews that you have a tendency to side w/your friends instead of representing your clients to your utmost abilities..this is something you're always done in your personal Life and has apparently carried over to your professional Life. Apparently you don't know the difference between a fact or grievance (from the D.Cortes review)....yes you have never represented us(me or Mr.Cortes) nor do I want you to....
Next fact; as an ARMY Veteran of Operation Iraq Freedom, that actually earned a pin..(cross rifles) earning the title of ARMY Infantryman...Please acknowledge the fact that you have not earned the title of Marine....as in present T.V. commercials it is still required to earn your EGA(Eagle, Globe and Anchor) pin to claim the title of Marine..it is to my understanding you yourself have made several post i.e Happy Birthday to my Fellow Marines....
As to the review of Annika S you favored her husband, (a Marine) due to the fact you were claiming to also be a Marine..you were at most a recruit in the Marine Corp, I give you that, you were hurt during boot camp training, didn't graduate and now go around claiming the title of Marine...we have terms for this in the ARMY such as, broke D--ks and sick call Rangers and since you never earned your EGA(Eagle Globe and Anchor) pin...Stop claiming you were ever a Marine...!!!
In typical Eric W/Lawyer like behavior you failed to Acknowledge the factual review(D.Cortes)about you..
We do not need your contact #'s this does not need to be talked about...the facts need to be acknowledged by you Eric W and shared with future clients (not a Marine) and to Quit siding with opposing council....
You will not hear from me,  because in your false compassion to Mr.Cortes (whatever you're struggling thru at this point in Your Life) I, nor Mr.Cortes are struggling thru anything in Life.....

reviews.text: Mr. Symonds was extremely helpful. Though he does not practice any longer in the field that I am in need of, he took the time to speak with me and gave me excellent advice.  He did not have to do this and he did not charge me for his time.  This is truly A1 service and it is apparent he is an excellent attorney and a more than qualified attorney.  If I need an attorney in the future for the services he offers, he will be my first call.

My experience with Mr. Symonds was a complete nightmare! He was found via text message by opposing council. Once in his office, I found out that they are close enough to know the opposing council’s husband’s birthday by heart. Clearly this raised some red flags right from the beginning. However, proving things like that are very difficult in Court. Mr. Symonds, who was assigned to be the minors attorney, did not interview the minor's therapist who was involved with the case for the previous 4 years! Instead he kept telling me his own story how he got full custody of his own kids via a 730 evaluation. He clearly, in my observation, is still very angry at his ex who now has passed away and was unable to disassociate his past history from my current case. He also showed bias towards my ex, who was a former Marine just like Mr. Symonds. In my opinion he prides himself in his fee of $150/hour and he told me that now, since he is a minors attorney, the Judges have to listen to him, where they didn’t before. Mr. Symonds did not follow up with me about the case at all. I prepared  a binder to show all of the e-mail communication between me and my ex. Mr. Symonds did not even read it, he seemed to have already sided with my ex as soon as he discovered they were both former marines, completely unprofessional!  At one point he told me that I did not “please him”, this was behind closed doors of course. I contacted a paralegal in regards to filing a formal complaint with the bar, she said that filing the bar complaint would only put him through some ethics classes. Thankfully he is off the case now. The 730 evaluation was completely in my favor. Mr. Symonds incompetence and bias caused many people to suffer. It took a 730 evaluator who had 30+ years to step in to resolve the case. Mr. Symonds has very little experience. Please save yourself heartache and stay as far away of him as possible.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Annika,

I hope this finds you well.

I never represented you, I represented your children. I understand that you were not happy with some of the things that I had to say. I am sorry that you are so bitter toward me that you have chosen to malign me in this way. What you have written here will potentially affect my livelihood. It is undeserved and, frankly, unbecoming.

To whoever should happen to read this, please understand that there are always two sides to every story. If you are reading this, it probably means that you have a pending Family Law matter. Just as is probably the case with your “ex,” Annika is taking small truths and twisting them. Annika has chosen to attack me here because she obviously doesn’t like me. It was not my job for her to like me, and I definitely told her things she did not want to hear.

Please see my other reviews, left for me by people whom I have actually represented.

Respectfully,
Eric W. Symonds, Esq.

We used them for an eviction.  We had two unmarried occupants we were trying to get rid of.  On the first court date of the eviction process, one of the two declared bankruptcy so we had to go to federal court to clear the eviction process.  This turned out to be a delaying tactic as the tenant never filed for bankruptcy.  During the first court date at the time we were speaking with the judge, our attorney miscalculated the amount of rent due by several hundred dollars.  We caught him and had a flurry of back and forth and the attorney gave the corrected amount. We reconvened when the federal judge authorized eviction.  When we reconvened we knew that the tenant did NOT file for bankruptcy.  Our attorney got a money judgment against the first tenant but not the second.  He FORGOT!  Now we can't sue the primary wage earner because they FORGOT to get a judgment.  They are not returning our calls.  WE DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS FIRM.  We now can not collect back rent from the primary wage earner who turned out to be smarter than our attorneys!!!

I needed an Estate Planninh Attorney due to the death of my husband.  I wanted our Living Trust reviewed so that I could make some changes. If I could give Yeager Law a 6 star review in every category, I'd do it.  After much discussion with free probate attorneys in the LA County area, I hired this law firm to open a probate case when my mother passed away.
Typically, you would get a free 15 minute consultation with a law firm; Michael Yeager spent 90 minutes on the phone with me. I was immediately impressed with his knowledge, demeanor, and his willingness to answer all my questions without feeling rushed. We discussed the pros and cons of opening a Heggstad vs. Probate case (It's a long story) , and after much deliberation, it was mutually agreed upon, to proceed with a probate case. I made a decision to sell my mom's condo, and as it turns out, I needed to probate her real property, based on the fact that 50% of the property was held in a Living Trust ( I am the Successor Trustee) and 50% was held in her name only. My Step-Dad had passed away prior to my mom's death, and he had bequeathed his half to her. After his death, my mom and I, failed to put that half back in the Trust. Even though probate cases are much more expensive vs. a Heggstad case ( Statutory Attorney fees are set the State of California, not the lawyer) I had to do it.
If your needing a probate lawyer in the greater Los Angeles area, I highly recommend Yeager Law. Every time I called this firm, they answered my questions on the spot. Jennifer (Michael Yeager's highly efficient and knowledgably paralegal) was always  so friendly and nice) If I sent them an e-mail, it was handled, usually within 30 minutes (not like other firms stating we will get back to you within 24 to 48 hours). If you want personalized attention, and the expertise of the Industry, hire Yeager Law! In the near future, I will be hiring Yeager Law to open up a Living Trust for my wife and I, so my daughter won't have to go through what I just went through with my mom's passing.

We just completed our estate planning for the second time with Yeager Law. We found this firm to be very efficient, knowledgeable, easy to work with & reasonably priced. We highly recommend using them for your estate planning & probate needs.

reviews.text:
I was chosen by the family to act as administrator of the family estate.  I was looking for a probate lawyer and didn't know how to best choose one when a card arrived via mail introducing Yeager Law.  I called and made an appointment.  After I left the appointment, I knew that I had our attorney, and I was really right.  Mike and Yeager Law have been a Godsend.  Not only were Mike and his staff very professional, friendly and most helpful, Mike himself was unlike any attorney that I had ever met.  Not once did I ever feel like I was on a production line being rushed through my appointment.  From our first meeting, he took time to go through the probate process while answering all of my questions without that rushed feeling.  In fact, there were times, when I had to drop by and sign some paperwork.  If I had any questions, and he wasn't busy with a client, he would take the time to answer my questions or address any issues to my satisfaction.  I never left with any questions or issues on my mind.  As a result, I was able to relieve my family of their fears and apprehensions.  This reassurance has continued throughout the probate.
What really makes a difference is that we have already received the court's approval of our petition for distribution.  It has only been one year.  It would have been much sooner, but the court procedures have changed requiring more time to complete the process.  I know that if I had tried to go it alone, it would have taken a lot longer than one year.  Not only that, Mike has been educating me as we have gone through each stage of the probate process, and I am a whole lot more informed than when I first started the process.
From day one, I have trusted and felt confident in Mike and his law firm's ability to get me through the probate, and he has never done anything to cause me to change my mind.  I would recommend and have recommended him to family, friends, and strangers.
When one considers the reason for probate and the loss that one has suffered, it really helps to have an understanding, compassionate law firm.  And and this has truly been a very trying time for me and my family as we lost both parents within two months of each other.  Working with Yeager Law has really helped us get through this process as easily as we have.  This has been a direct result of their friendliness, courtesy, respect, and desire to help us during this most painful period of grieving.
There's a final perk to this probate, not only did I find a really excellent attorney, but I have made some new friends.  This was most unexpected by me, and I am most grateful for the added bonus.

I initially made an appt to create a trust for myself and a family member. Michael was, initially, friendly. The "free" consultation turned in to " you should get something started now."  Trusting him to get something done, we signed the contract and paid several hundred dollars for something that should have been super simple (which wasn't what we initially wanted but okay).

The following week Michael claimed he could not locate the information needed for our home. No matter the documentation provided he insisted that the information was not located in the system and we didn't have enough proof. My relative made one phone call and retrieved the information and where he could locate it. I informed Michael of this and he insisted that we were wrong. I asked him if was unable to complete the task or if it was out of his scope. He became defense and suggested terminating our contract with a refund...and no referrals.

Upon receipt of my refund, there was a nice nasty letter insisting that he was right and that we were wrong. My family member and I are both highly educated professionals who trusted another (presumed) professional to provide a service. Instead, we receive a condescending attitude from someone who couldn't admit that they didn't know what they were doing and refer us elsewhere.

Note: Ask your friends for referrals or just try to do it yourself.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I’m truly sorry I couldn’t take care of your matter the way you wanted. The advice and recommended course of action I provide at any initial consultation is always based on the information I receive during that consultation. Given the nature of estate planning, and the consequences of inaction, I often recommend that people get started immediately. As later facts and details emerge, I may change my advice and recommended course of action, which is what I did in this case. Once it was determined that the the level of service you wanted could not be provided in the manner you expected, I terminated the contract and issued a full refund, not even charging for the time and research I put into your matter. I couldn’t provide a referral because, in my professional opinion, I don’t personally know any attorney who could assist you based on the later discovered facts.

Although you may not think positively of your experience with me, I sincerely hope that you are able to get your matter resolved effectively with whomever you feel most comfortable.

reviews.text: The service I received was caring, pleasant and thorough.  Michael was able to tailor my Trust to fit my current needs (with a teenage son) but also make provisions for when he is old enough to be my successor Trustee; so that my Trust grows with my child.  I didn't even know that was possible, I was expecting to have to revise my Trust in a few years.  THIS is why an attorney is a better value than a legal aid service.  I know my son's interests are protected now and in the future.

I definitely can’t say enough good things about Mr.Yeager ... Definitely great legal help for a fair price. After my fathers passing another attorney who turned his practice over to another attorney that starts with a V... on the Blvd wanted a bunch of $$$ to even talk about our issues with our  family trust that their firm did in 99.., after everything was said and done they claimed that they don’t keep important documents aka Trust and Wills only a few years....from here on out Michael is our guy...I definitely appreciate the level of service we received  and recommend the firm highly to anyone.

I had a consultation with Mike about a family issue and he was able to give us a map of all the steps necessary to resolve the issue. His professionalism, knowledge and clarity about the field is definitely a highlight worth noting for your needs.

I BELEIVE THAT MR. YEAGER HAS A CERTAIN NUMBER OF YEARS OF EXPERIENCE PRACTICING LAW IN THE AREA SUCH AS TRUSTS AND ESTATES . THEREFORE TO MAKE AN APPOINTMENT YOU CAN HAVE A CONSULTATION.
I RECOMMEND FAMILY AND FRIENDS FOR MR. MICHAEL YEAGER PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE. MY EXPERIENCE MR. MICHAEL YEAGER IS STILL FRESH IN MY MEMORY, HE IS THE BEST CONFIDENTIAL ATTORNEY I HAVE MET.

I am aware that there are both good attorneys and bad attorneys, Steers & Associates is beyond good.  They delivered excellent results and were sensitive to all of my needs. Highly recommend this law firm.

I am a Black American male and someone who is recognized as a respected Father and business professional. I am a man god, educated, loving-protective-providing father whom does have and has expressed custodial interest and 50/50 custody of my son.

I had hired Pat back in Aug of 2023.
Shortly after I hired Pat, I was involved in a dmv situation in front of my minor son. My ex who committed the DMV was arrested and I received temporary custody of my son at the time.

Fast forward Over the past year from Aug 2023 through July 2024 I attended only one court hearing with Pat Murphy in which Pat did not bring up the CRITICAL dmv situation or use clear evidence of the dmv video, witness statements and etc confirming the actions that took place.
This would have helped with getting custody of my son.

I also requested for a restraining (Which I did not receive) and primary custody of my son.

Rather than what I requested and what should be entitled to me by law.

Minutes before the court hearing Pat tells me that I need to agree to the other lawyer terms and request because if I didn’t the judge would look at me as being unfair and could sway opinion and influence judgment of decision.

Pat was counter productive. She was more in alignment with the lawyer Timothy and not concerned with what the laws and ensuring I had equal rights of protection.

Pat irresponsibly negotiated a way for me to pay spousal support, continue with visitation every other weekend and to my ex when in fact spousal support is not paid to the other party when there is a restraining order in place.

That was not the support I was requesting.

Then Pat decides to sub out of my case after I call her out from ignoring my emails and text messages for weeks and I was demanding for better representation from her especially with all the evidence I had.

This lady is terrible.

Best lawyer in town! An epitome of customer service After 18 years of my living trust she still had a copy of my living trust. And did not charge me for the 106 pages she made. She is amazing! That's honestly the best word to describe her. She has been helping me with variety of cases and the most important one regarding my children. She has always answered me and my children's questions and always makes time. I know she is very busy but understand that she assists as many people as she can to help where she is needed!! I love her as an attorney and a person. She has truly changed all my family's life for the best. Thank you so much for all you have done. Trust me thank you doesn't even cover it!!!!! :)
Karma will catch up .  I'll  patiently wait.

Update! Murphy lies even when telling the truth is easier. Plaintiff's attorney states Murphy did not cooperate in any way on my case for over 2 1/2 years!  LONG BEFORE HER ILLNESS! Ignored him. Ignored me. She should lose her license. Refused to meet, answer communications, or try to resolve my matter with him. I was later abandoned by her because of blatant malpractice, as court was in September 2024, and no way could she face me, judge, or Plaintiff's attorney, after ignoring my case, and utter inertia! Elder abuse. Malpractice. Abuse of power. Be scared. Be VERY scared. Too "sick" to practice, yet her office remains open. Karma can be hard.
reviews.ownerResponse: Due to very serious health issues (cancer) that this client was informed of (as was plaintiffs counsel) this client was notified last July that I would not be able to continue to handle her matter. I understand she was upset to be informed of my inability to continue on her behalf but that response and subsequent communications from her while I was undergoing treatment that were similar in tone to this review does not make her statements true.

reviews.text: Ms. Murphy has shared excellent advice with me and was a strong advocate for my case. I have nothing but the utmost regard for her judgment. She is well known and respected in Kern County by the judges and other attorneys.

Be sure Pat Murphy is OK and taking cases. The first time I used her she was superb, answered communications, and won my case. This time I have contacted her repeatedly, when is the court date, and a few other very important questions,  by voice mail, email, and fax FOR TWO WEEKS. No response. I realize things can happen but a response takes a few minutes and 1) STOPS repeat communications from concerned and scared clients and 2) shows good character and business acumen. I am not well and have to drive to Lancaster from Bakersfield to ask peace officers to do a wellness check. She could be on her office floor comatose or dead. Or being held captive, as this DOES happen to women. Most clients would not care. I do.

UPDATE: Murphy abandoned me. Backed out of my September 17, 2024 court date. Told me to contact her September 30, 2024 for new court date and time, and replacement attorney. Mine is a simple email to answer. Has, as is typical, ignored me. The irony is she displays the exact same behavior as the Plaintiff. Abuse of power, and ignoring legitimate questions. Then whines I send "too many" emails.  Be scared. Be VERY scared. How a professional handles personal and professional things during adversity, speaks volumes. TOLD ME SHE HAS TO BACK OFF HER PRACTICE, YET SHOWS AS OPEN EXCEPT THURSDAYS.

reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Negrete and his family were not clients. His parents were litigants in a guardianship case in which the court appointed me minors counsel. That case went on for over 2 years and multiple petitions.  When the negretes were satisfied with the court's orders they posted no review. When they were not happy with the outcome they posted this. I try to do the best job i can on every case i am appointed on and try to be fair and respectful to all. However as minors counsel my job is to represent the best interests of my client, the child. Doing that job does not require that i do it the way other litigants want me to but rather as i see fit for the benefit of my client. I recognize that concept is difficult at times for families to accept since there is a lot of emotion involved in these cases.  Ultimately the decision is up to the court. This case has been over for many months. The timing of this review is curious given that fact and the fact that i always responded to their emails when i received them including the  one i received yesterday requesting a copy of the conformed order from the last hearing. I wish their family well and hope that all can learn to get along for the sake of the child who is in the middle of all the people who profess to love her but who cant seem to figure out how to get along for her sake.

reviews.text: If you need an attorney who is experienced dedicated hardworking at a very reasonable and fair rate, Pat Murphy is the the right woman for the job. reviews.text: Worst office EVER! I retained her for a Disability case and she NEVER SENT IN THE PAPERWORK! When I called they would say they were still waiting for a response. After 11 months I called the SSA myself to inquire and found out my case was closed due to not receiving my request for appeal. When I called to ask about it they were unable to provide the supposed tracking info on the paperwork. Then when I tried to call them again they kept hanging up on me. RUN AWAY FROM HERE AS FAST AS YOU CAN! There are plenty of competent lawyers in the AV. Look elsewhere for help.

I retained Pat Murphy last October. I saw her twice (initial free consult and to sign the petition) and paid her $1000 retainer and almost $500 to file a petition for divorce - uncontested and no property or kids - I have never heard from her since. I have gone to her office 4 times and called more tan 15 times to date. She is never there and the office girls (different one almost every time) tell me they will leave messages or said that they sent her a text that I had called to get any information on my case. The court cashed my check on 12/24 and filed the petition on 12/27. But NOTHING has been done since. NOTHING! I am at a loss because she has never returned any of my calls or been in her office any time I have gone in. I've never heard of this type of issue with any other lawyer making $1000 to file a 2 page document and then never getting back to their client. I'm out that money and STILL married!!!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: I believe I know which client this is and she is correct that it has taken longer than either one of us anticipated to get her case done. This is partly due to the fact that in January my husband went in for surgery and ended up being hospitalized for 4 months due to a stroke which did disrupt my ability to attend to my practice as closely as I would have liked. During the same time frame I lost my part-time employee who had been with me for 10 years but who had gone to CNA school and gotten a job in that field and my full-time employee very unexpectedly due to health problems of her own. Contrary to how this situation is described it was not just a simple divorce because of the fact that the other party essentially disappeared and in order to get the divorce service has to be accomplished. That involves investigation and service attempts and ultimately publication. I have spoken with this client since this review was posted and explained to her what was going on so hopefully she has a little better understanding that I was not ignoring her or any other of my clients during a difficult period in my life.

reviews.text:
My case was a challenging one. My income was not low, but I definitely needed financial relief due to pending divorce and other circumstances. In other words, I did not have a "cookie cutter" situation.

I was referred to this office by my family law attorney. From the very beginning, I found the office very easy professional and easy to communicate with.

There came a point in the process where there was additional documentation and work needed. My attorney (Greg) called me and explained the situation. I will be honest that I was frustrated, but later realized that Greg was using his experience to do the "right thing" on my case. And then when I got the bill for the extra work, it was deeply discounted and more than fair (fair to me, probably not the office).

In the end, my debt was discharged. I'm not sure that would have happened without the experience of this office.

I got the sense that Greg was doing his job, but also sincerely had empathy for my situation.

So, if you do not have a cookie cutter situation, and want an emphatic and very competent office, I recommend Wadhwani and Shanfeld.

He also gave us some options on steps of action we could take on getting this debt settled and/or cleared with this creditor. Fresh Start for me!!!! After 2-3 months of waiting, it is finally over!! If you owe anything over $40k in credit card debts, CALL these guys... But before you do, always go for a non-profit money managers to see if Bankruptcy is your only choice, in my case it is(according to 3 non-profit organization). I called them, they've assigned me a lawyer, the lawyer will ask a lot of questions, but easy enough for you to answer. I also asked a LOT of questions, because I don't want to make mistakes. They helped me throughout the whole process.

Pros:
Cheaper than the other lawyers I've asked (by roughly $500)
You can deposit just $100 and hire them(but need to complete all fee's to proceed the filing)

Cons:
Have multiple location/offices, but need to schedule an appointment (you can't just drop in the office near you (not a big deal though, for me)

Overall:
Excellent Outcome

reviews.text: Whatever you do please do not waste your time nor resources using this firm. Not only will they pretend to help you, they will also take your money and waste everyone’s time in court. They will illegally sign your name on documents and fail to show up when you pay them to.

Navigating a child custody and visitation case is one of the most stressful experiences a parent can face, but working with Ms. Chellei Jimenez made all the difference. From the very beginning, they demonstrated incredible expertise in family law and a deep understanding of my unique situation. Ms. Jimenez was always accessible, answering my questions promptly and providing clear, honest guidance throughout the process.

What truly stood out was her compassion and dedication—not only were they a fierce advocate for my rights, but they genuinely cared about ensuring the best outcome for my child. Thanks to their hard work and strategic approach, I was able to secure a fair custody arrangement and visitation schedule that prioritizes my child’s well-being.

If you’re looking for a skilled, compassionate, and highly professional family lawyer, I cannot recommend Ms. Jimenez enough. They turned a challenging and emotional process into a manageable one, and I’ll always be grateful for their help.”*

I had a concerning experience with Chellei Jimenez, whom I retained near the end of 2021. She deviated from our initial discussions and altered the agreed-upon objectives. I received unprofessional emails from her and her staff, particularly Claudia, who made numerous mistakes in filing paperwork. Despite my repeated attempts to explain the nature of the domestic violence I have been dealing with for years, my concerns were not adequately addressed. In fact, her paralegal Claudia found it laughable, stating "Welcome to  California". Another employee she claimed to have fired after she aggressively tried to make me sign papers full of false information about my finances. Ironically, I now have a restraining order against the mother based on the same evidence I previously presented to Chellei. This same evidence I presented to Chellei was one of her reasons in requesting a motion to withdraw. Our judge granted the restraining order without a hearing, indicating that my papers and concerns had merit.

Unfortunately, my daughter has been diagnosed with an emotional disturbance disorder, after the same events I expained to Chellei kept happening. Chellei's approach exacerbated the situation, increasing conflict and casting me as the antagonist. She advised me to dismiss serious issues (see screenshot), although they eventually led a judge in California to issue a restraining order against the mother. Despite successfully fighting a domestic violence restraining order about three years ago, Chellei's decision to request a short-cause custody trial, which I believe was a critical mistake, has had long-lasting effects on my relationship with my daughter.

Chellei Jimenez never informed me about a very important section code in California Family Law.  I learned about Section Code 3044 during my 2nd year in law school, which pertains to domestic violence.

Our honorable judge is now questioning why it took me so long to bring these issues up (screenshot below). The delay was due to Chellei's tactics, which ultimately reduced my custodial time with my daughter by 80%. Two other attorneys have notified me of her mistakes. One of them is very familiar with Chellei as a colleague. After I sent her an email with evidence of the abuse, she downplayed it and filed a motion for withdrawal, claiming the information was irrelevant to my case.  (It was used as an exhibit and restraining order was granted recently with another attorney)

After two years of law school and several consultations, I've been validated in my belief that her tactics hurt my case tremendously. I hope we can work toward a remedy and implement additional ethical guidelines for attorneys who claim to be specialized and certified in family law.

When retaining an attorney who claims to be certified as a family law specialists with decades of experience, especially when it involves your children, you should have peace of mind knowing your investment is in capable hands.

Best wishes to all the dedicated mothers and fathers who simply want to be involved in their children's lives.

I invite an open dialogue to explore her actions while representing me and also to help validate my review. I have never wrote an in-depth 1 star review until now. I hope this post helps a single mother or father out there in making an informed decision.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to leave a review. We are truly sorry to hear that you feel disappointed with your experience.

Family law matters are often emotional and stressful, and we understand how difficult the process can be for clients and their families. Our office works hard to provide compassionate, professional representation while also giving honest guidance about what the court can and cannot do.

Because confidentiality rules prevent us from discussing the details of any case in a public forum, we are unable to respond to the specific statements made in your review. However, we take client concerns seriously and would welcome the opportunity to speak with you directly to address any misunderstandings or unresolved issues.

If you would like to discuss this further, please contact our office so we may review your concerns. We wish you the best moving forward.

My questions were answered promptly, my documents were filed quickly and overall they made this dreaded experience a walk in the park.  Don't be discouraged by the fake reviews posted on here about this office, I took a chance and got excellent results.  I hope you do too.  Bottom line is, Mr.Hernandez is a shark. reviews.text:
Samantha E. deserves every bit of praise we can give. From the very beginning of our case, she was organized, responsive, and completely in command of every detail. What could have felt overwhelming and stressful was instead handled with calm confidence and clear direction.

She worked seamlessly with our insurance adjuster, navigating conversations and negotiations with ease. At no point did we feel uncertain about where things stood. Samantha communicated consistently, explained everything thoroughly, and ensured we felt informed and supported throughout the entire process. She handled every interaction with professionalism, poise, and a level of competence that truly set her apart.

Beyond her expertise, Samantha treated us with genuine respect and care. She never made us feel like just another case file. Her dedication, efficiency, and attention to detail made a difficult situation far more manageable than we ever expected.

Samantha E. is truly an asset to R. Rex Parris Law Firm. Her professionalism, knowledge, and commitment to her clients reflect incredibly well on the firm as a whole. We are deeply grateful for her hard work and would confidently recommend her to anyone seeking exceptional legal support.

We also want to recognize R. Rex Parris himself. He went above and beyond to help us with our case, demonstrating not only legal expertise but genuine care and commitment. His leadership and dedication were evident every step of the way. It is clear why so many people trust him both as an attorney and as Mayor of Lancaster.

We highly recommend R. Rex Parris Law Firm to anyone in need of strong, reliable legal representation. Our experience was outstanding from start to finish, and we fully support Mr. Parris in his continued service to the community as Lancaster’s Mayor.

reviews.text:
Do not recommend this Attorney.
He took my $5000 retainer and didn't do anything for my case. I am still in the same position. He kept pushing my court date out over and over again. Now demanding another retainer.

Hire someone else. Very crooked.

Mr. Johnson was very helpful throughout the process. He represented my ex-wife thru the divorce. I used no attorney, I was treated fairly and feel like I was given an opportunity to address my concerns.
I loved working with this entire office. Tim Johnson took on my case quickly, was thorough, prepared and extremely helpful.

Unprofessional and very condescending. Very rude and did not come through with anything after they received payment. Secretary will lie and claim confrontation instead of returning your calls. I wouldn't recommend this law firm
reviews.ownerResponse:
Mr. Brown,
I am sorry you feel that way. However, we both know your comments aren’t accurate. You waited until the last minute to retain me. I gave you explicit instructions on what I needed you to do to allow me to be prepared to help you in such short time and informed you exactly what I would do and when I would do it. You failed to do your part but I did mine. When my staff reiterated what I needed from you, it was you who was rude and disrespectful, including hanging up the phone on me and the staff. All I communicated to you was that I truly wanted to help you but in order for us to work together I asked that you be respectful to my staff. You took issue with my request for mutual respect and we agreed it was best we parted ways. You forgot to mention I didn’t charge you ANYTHING for the work that my staff and I did do and I issued you a full refund immediately after you signed the paperwork relieving me as you attorney. I also called you within minutes of you calling and asking to talk to me. So your comment about calls being returned isn’t true at all.

To those reading this review and response, my staff and I are highly aware that when you call our office it is likely because you are involved in a stressful and difficult situation. This can result in people who are short with us and frustrated with what they are dealing with. We truly want to help you in a difficult time and understand you may be short on patience. But being frustrated and being rude and disrespectful are two different things. Another take away that is important, is that if you want your attorney to be prepared to assist you and they ask you for information, provide it in a timely manner so they can be prepared and fight for you like you deserve. This office WILL fight for you.

I am a young mom dealing with a horrifying situation no parent should have to go through. He has guided me in the right direction and hope you give him the chance to listen and you also receive constructive criticism. Thank you Timothy!

I contacted Tim Johnson to assist me with some issues that I was running into with the father of my children. I wasn't able to resolve these matters on my own, so I needed some guidance.

I found Tim to be highly educated, organized, easy to talk to, and readily available to answer my endless questions. He was very upfront and realistic with what to expect with my case.

Thankfully my case went smoothly and the judgement was in my favor. Tim always kept mine and my children's best interests at heart and fought for us. He's a father as well, so I trusted him every step of the way.
Very professional and knowledgeable! Thank you for the great advice!!!
It's nice to be represented by an attorney that knows what he's doing!

Do not use him he did not fight for me I came in with 98% of my documents completed  he copied them and billed me for the same documents I gave him .recommend I leave my home after I showed proof my ex-wife lived somewhere else when I realized he was not trying to fight for me he wanted more and more money I decided  I can fight for my self and told him I no longer needed his services  he was in court with my ex-wife  attorney supporting  her I couldn't believe what I was seeing he is shady don't trust him
Tim Johnson was attentive and professional and answered all my questions in a timely manner. I felt he had my best interests in mind.  Tim and all his staff were available when I needed them.

My case ended in 2015. I was represented by Tim. I interviewed him because it was important to me that I was hiring the right person for the job not the lawyer wan ting a case. Tim was straight, to the point and that is what I like. None of this beating around the bush to get an extra buck out of me. I was the one to ask the questions during the interview and he listened and answered. Sometimes as people we only see one thing when it comes to a case and that is all for us but when you are wrong you are wrong and some people cannot handle it. I know some people can't handle bluntness but that is the best way to handle a case in my opinion. Lawyer is not hiding anything from you or holding back. Tim even tried to back track what I was owed and even though it was not possible because the law prevented it, and I wish it could have been, I respected it because the law was right and it was not in my favor. It happens. We are not always right on what we think we are going to get due to, unfortunately, other matters that come into play. In the end all my questions were answered, nothing was hidden, straight and to the point. If you cannot handle a lawyer that is being blunt honest with everything in your case without you getting butt hurt then this is not the lawyer for you. Tim did his job, he got me what corresponded to me, not what I thought that did, according to the law. I really thank him and the firm for everything. Even the receptionist ladies at the front desk. A great job well done.

I hired the Johnson Law Firm because they were recommended to me by a friend and their initial retainer was a $1000 less than another lawyer that I regret to this day not hiring. Hiring the Johnson Law Firm has been one of the worse decisions I have ever made. My initial consultation was with Robert Johnson, the experienced lead partner of the firm. However, unfortunately for me, he turned out to be quite unscrupulous and appeared to only play the role of bait to lure me in with his legal knowledge and powerful presence. He failed to be available for consequent meetings and I received a letter notifying me of his retirement less than a month after my initial meeting with him and their collection of my hefty retainer. My case was filed and handled by his very young and inexperienced nephew, Tim Johnson. I was left having paid top dollar for a newbie that had a chip on his shoulder and wanted a gold star for just showing up to my hearing. Among the multitude of ineptitudes of Tim Johnson was that on the day of my hearing it appeared that he had not so much as read my husband's income and expense declaration, let alone actually prepared for my case. He could not have done a worse job. I am disabled and by the time my hearing came, I had been without any income or spousal support for close to a year. Tim was neither empathetic to my situation or prepared to represent me. He was deceptive in his explanations of issues on a settlement agreement he wanted me to agree to, as well as, in his negotiations with my spouse and his lawyer. He had failed to meet with me prior my hearing for any length of time and was really not interested in knowing the details of my case. He showed up completely unprepared and lacking any knowledgeable of even the basic details of my case. What's worse, his errors set a precedence at future hearings. Thanks to Tim Johnson my husband is continuing to not pay court ordered spousal support even though I have $600 monthly out-of-pocket medical expenses and he makes $250,000 per year. He did not help me in receiving a support amount on all my husband's income and failed to get a almost mandatory stipulation on my support order to garnish my husband's wages. Now my husband has even won all the proceeds from the sale of my marital home due to something Tim misled me on at my first hearing. I don't even have money for groceries. Tim and all high level staff at Johnson Law Firm are not concerned with you, your case, or your future. They are only interested in their pockets and how much they can make off you by expelling the least amount of hours and work- and don't even think about calling them with a question or two. You might inconvenience them...
They did an amazing job, Samantha was a great help with my entire case

reviews.text: I cannot say I had a great experience with Mr. Wallin’s team. I hired Mr. Wallin to defend me with some trouble I got in and I’ll be honest even though the outcome was better then if I had stuck with the “Public Pretender” it wasn’t from any work my attorney from Mr. Wallin’s team had done. Everytime we went to court it seemed like he had no idea who I was and how to handle my case. All he did was kick it out a month or 2 everytime. I guess hoping for the best as far as the DA giving in. But with no strategy on what we could do to make the DA lower their plea offer. In the end it all came down to my judge who was an awesome judge who didn’t want to put me away for the crime I was charged with. That and the public defender that represented my co-defendant pushed to have the outcome we had. It was funny how my attorney with Mr. Wallin’s team was so excited when it was over like as if he did something! He did NOTHING! I settled for the best I felt I could get at that point. He didn’t do anything to assure me of getting anything better after paying $5500!! While my co-defendant payed $0 for his public pretender and we both got same outcome.
Extremely professional, attention to detail was second to none. Highly recommend.

Dear Atty. Chris, Cristina PA

I hope this note finds you well. I wanted to take a moment to express my deepest gratitude for all your hard work, dedication, and expertise throughout my legal matter. Your exceptional guidance and unwavering support have made a significant impact on the positive outcome of my case.

From our very first meeting, you exhibited a level of professionalism that immediately put me at ease. Your attention to detail and thorough understanding of the law reassured me that I was in capable hands. Your ability to explain complex legal concepts in a clear and concise manner was truly impressive.

Beyond your professional expertise, I am grateful for your friendly and compassionate demeanor. You consistently made yourself available to address my concerns and patiently answered every question I had. Your genuine care for your clients truly sets you apart. I feel incredibly fortunate to have had you as my legal advocate.

Please accept my sincerest thanks for your exceptional representation, unwavering support, and friendship throughout this challenging process. Your expertise and guidance have truly made a difference, and I will not hesitate to recommend you to others in need of legal assistance.

Warmest regards,

Jennifer

My license wouldn’t be an issue. It wasn’t until a little over a couple days ago that I received papers in the mail stating that my license was going to be suspended for 3 and a half months. Not to mention I had this DMV court hearing and wasn’t updated by his law firm nor was I aware of the outcome. When I had emailed the firm about this situation they had confirmed that I had lost my license. It was nothing to the staff or to my attorney I spent all of my savings on. Not to mention the other officer that received a DUI with me hired a totally different attorney and she still has her license,and she crashed into a center divider.
Btw I should have listened to all the bad reviews I seen before hiring him. He guaranteed that those weren’t accurate as well.

reviews.text:
My case was a challenging one. My income was not low, but I definitely needed financial relief due to pending divorce and other circumstances. In other words, I did not have a "cookie cutter" situation.

I was referred to this office by my family law attorney. From the very beginning, I found the office very easy professional and easy to communicate with.

There came a point in the process where there was additional documentation and work needed. My attorney (Greg) called me and explained the situation. I will be honest that I was frustrated, but later realized that Greg was using his experience to do the "right thing" on my case. And then when I got the bill for the extra work, it was deeply discounted and more than fair (fair to me, probably not the office).

In the end, my debt was discharged. I'm not sure that would have happened without the experience of this office.

I got the sense that Greg was doing his job, but also sincerely had empathy for my situation.

So, if you do not have a cookie cutter situation, and want an emphatic and very competent office, I recommend Wadhwani and Shanfeld.

reviews.text:
Fresh Start for me!!!! After 2-3 months of waiting, it is finally over!! If you owe anything over $40k in credit card debts, CALL these guys... But before you do, always go for a non-profit money managers to see if Bankruptcy is your only choice, in my case it is(according to 3 non-profit organization). I called them, they've assigned me a lawyer, the lawyer will ask a lot of questions, but easy enough for you to answer. I also asked a LOT of questions, because I don't want to make mistakes. They helped me throughout the whole process.

Pros:
Cheaper than the other lawyers I've asked (by roughly $500)
You can deposit just $100 and hire them(but need to complete all fee's to proceed the filing)

Cons:
Have multiple location/offices, but need to schedule an appointment (you can't just drop in the office near you (not a big deal though, for me)

Overall:
Excellent Outcome

reviews.text: Best lawyer ever ..Mr funn helped get a old case removed and his prices are very reasonable...thanks Mr fenn...frm Alphonso Nedd...

Mr. Fenn surprised me several times.

First, he answered his phone late in the afternoon,  at a time that not only shows he appreciates that his clientele are working class folks but that he is not afraid of working hard himself...he was doing the dishes when I called.

Secondly,  Robert was very knowledgeable and gave great legal advice of how I could handle the 100+ speeding ticket (my first ticket in a decade and the bike doesn't feel like it's going that fast =‐)) on my own. He said "this is how I would handle your case, you cant get out of this ticket, the best you could hope for is not to have your license suspended..." I'm paraphrasing. He also told me I could do it without him the same way, he told me what paperwork to file and where to file it.

Third, he surprised me with his affordability. Time is money and him taking on the case was the smartest move even if my license was suspended and I had to pay a $1000 fine.

Lastly,  HE HAD MY TICKET DISMISSED.  He over-delivered and under-promised!

By the way, Officer Shields is doing great work on HWY 138.

This was my first time ever needing a lawyer in my life. What I enjoyed the most about him and as his honesty. He didn’t beat around the bush and got straight to the point with my case. He was very knowledgeable about the traffic violation I was fighting and was able to be realistic with me regarding my case. He even let me know that if had the time I could fight it myself. However I knew that I didn’t want to fight it myself because I was scared of messing things up in court. So I enlisted the professional help from this law firm. We are extremely satisfied with the service at Thompson Von Tungeln Trust & Will Law Office. First, we scheduled and attended their seminar. It was informative. I can’t say enough good things about the attorneys and staff at Thompson | Von Tungeln. They handled both my family trust and my daughter’s conservatorship, and the level of professionalism and care they showed was outstanding.

They made sure every detail was handled correctly, and most importantly, they helped me set up a special needs trust for my daughter, Ava, so I can feel confident she’ll be taken care of for life. Their compassion and understanding made a very emotional process so much easier.

I also attended one of their estate planning seminars, which was incredibly informative. They covered everything from charitable giving and tax-saving strategies to trusts for families with special needs. I learned things I had never heard from other law firms — including how property owners can support charities and receive significant tax benefits through charitable remainder trusts and similar planning tools.

If you’re looking for attorneys who are both experienced and genuinely caring, I highly recommend Thompson | Von Tungeln. They go above and beyond to educate their clients and make sure your family’s future is secure.

Understanding estate planning and knowing who to trust are two huge hurdles in “managing one’s affairs.”
Kevin VonTungeln and Sonia Chan Lee's firm were highly recommended by other professionals, and they did not disappoint.  Kevin was exceedingly responsive, patient in explaining the law and generated work far faster than most firms.  An excellent team.
The law is not always black and white, yet he helped cut through the issues and provided good counsel to our family.

After attending a seminar at the Thompson | Von Tungeln law office, we knew that our trust needed an overhaul.  The seminar presented a tremendous amount of information and we set up an appointment right away.  We are so pleased with Kevin Von Tungeln  and the work he did for us.  He explained the importance of having an iron clad trust  including applicable inheritance laws and options.  He led us through the changes that needed to be done and answered many questions.  In the end we have a trust that will stand in any court of law and our children will have little to worry.  As a side note, we learned that about every 3~4 years a trust should be reviewed/adjusted in accordance with any changes in the law.  I highly recommend Thompson|Von Tungeln for all your estate planning needs.
